message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette param to Image.quantize() for custom palette color sorting after quantization

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add error_threshold parameter to quantize(). I need to edit Image.py (quantize method) and its test file. Please tell me which files you need so we can find a disjoint split, especially if you also need Image.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both touch quantize() in Image.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Let's share ownership and minimize conflict: I'll add my error_threshold param at the end of the quantize() signature (after existing params, before kmeans if that's last) and add my logic as an isolated block near the end of the method body, plus append new test functions at the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py without touching existing lines. Please do the same for sort_palette (append your param, isolated logic block, tests appended at end). We'll each commit independently and rely on the required git merge step to combine; since our edits touch different lines this should merge cleanly. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need Image.py's quantize() and its test file - can't be fully disjoint. Let's keep changes additive: I'll add a sort_palette param+docstring entry to quantize() without touching your error_threshold code, same for tests (new test functions, not editing yours). Whoever merges second will do a straightforward manual conflict resolution on the signature/docstring lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT

Proceeding to implement now.

note: Finished implementing error_threshold: added the parameter + docstring to quantize(), a new _quantize_to_error_threshold() helper method placed right after quantize() (before copy()), and 4 new test functions appended after test_quantize_dither_diff (before test_transparent_colors_equal) in Tests/test_image_quantize.py. All existing + new tests pass. Ready to merge whenever you are.

Note: our two helper methods land in the same spot (between quantize and copy) and the quantize() signature both got a new last param, so Image.py will need a manual merge conflict resolution - happy to do that on my end.
